# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

resources:
  - name: gce-instance-{{ deployment.name }}
    type: compute.v1.instance
    properties:
      zone: {{ properties['zone'] }}
      machineType: zones/{{ properties['zone'] }}/machineTypes/{{ properties['machineType'] }}
      disks:
        - deviceName: boot
          type: PERSISTENT
          boot: true
          autoDelete: true
          initializeParams:
            sourceImage: {{ properties['image'] }}
      networkInterfaces:
        - network: global/networks/default
          accessConfigs:
            - name: External NAT
              type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
      serviceAccounts:
        - email: {{ properties['serviceAccount'] }}
          scopes:
            - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform

imports:
  - path: ./my-template.yaml

parameters:
  - name: zone
    type: string
    description: "The zone for the GCE instance."
    default: "us-central1-a"
    
  - name: machineType
    type: string
    description: "The machine type for the GCE instance."
    default: "n1-standard-1"
    
  - name: image
    type: string
    description: "The source image for the boot disk."
    default: "projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-11"
    
  - name: serviceAccount
    type: string
    description: "The service account email to use."
    default: "default"
